title: Paid by the Ship
passage: For three centuries the small Baltic port of Vellmar entrusted its night-time safety to a guild of lantern-keepers, who were paid not by the town council but directly by the ships that used the harbour. A vessel that reached the quay safely paid the keepers a fixed toll; a vessel that ran aground paid nothing at all. The council's records show that it never once had to discipline a keeper for a darkened lantern. When the neighbouring town of Hesse-Aldern switched to salaried keepers funded from general taxation, its wreck rate climbed within a single decade, and its own chroniclers noted enviously that Vellmar's lamps never seemed to sleep. The Vellmar guild was finally dissolved in 1809, not for any failure of its own, but because a new state lighthouse authority quietly absorbed all of its duties.

questions.prompt: What does the passage suggest about why Vellmar's lanterns were so reliably kept lit?

options.text: The lamps stayed lit because the council never once had to discipline a keeper for a darkened lantern.
Baltic harbour towns kept stricter safety standards than inland ones, so Vellmar's lamps were bound to stay lit.
Private lantern guilds were always more effective than state-funded lighthouses, so Vellmar's could never fail.
Keepers were paid only when ships arrived safely, so keeping the lamps lit served their own interests.

questions.rationale: TYPE-002 inference one inch beyond the text. The passage states the payment structure (toll only on safe arrival, nothing on a wreck) and the outcome (never a dark lantern; the salaried rival's wrecks rose) but never names the incentive; D supplies exactly that one-step link. Every option is now cast in the same cause-and-effect shape, so the answer cannot be picked from surface form alone. A = reversed causality / too_literal (takes the recorded fact that a keeper was never disciplined as the cause, when it was itself a consequence of the lamps staying lit). B = two-step leap / outside_knowledge (unsupported regional generalisation that Baltic towns kept stricter standards). C = quantifier_upgrade ('always'/'never' over-broadens a single town-to-town comparison into a universal law).

generator_meta.originality_note: Vellmar, Hesse-Aldern, the guild and the quote 'never seemed to sleep' are all invented; 1809 date and 'state lighthouse authority' are generic period colour, no real person quoted.
